subject: Re: Allegations of Fraud - JC Household
body_text:
Sorry I’m on mobile, Waterleaf apartments wanted to know the voucher
amount, she is scheduled for a tour on Thursday and has already signed the
application and turned it in. Call her when you would like, but I’m
inclined to leave clackamas county despite the fear mongering, especially
about Julio coming back to live with us.

On Mon, Jan 12, 2026 at 9:59 AM benjamin barber <starworks5@gmail.com>
wrote:

> Jane doesn’t want to live with with Julio and has suggested that I take a
> restraining order against him on her behalf, and given that blossom told me
> the dog is not allowed and that the units would not be available that we
> chose the waterleaf apartments, and she has scheduled a tour of their place
> ( I went already) and a bus to take her there / ohsu campus , and I needed
> to confirm with waterless the amount of the voucher.
>
> However Jamila ( clackamas county employee)  been scaring jane about her
> losing section 8 if she leaves clackamas county
>
> On Mon, Jan 12, 2026 at 9:11 AM HACC - Relocation <
> HACC-Relocation@clackamas.us> wrote:
>
>> Hello,
>>
>> Here are the steps Jane will need to take to receive a Tenant Protection
>> Voucher and port to Multnomah County:
>>
>> 1.     Complete an intake packet and be screened by the intake team
>>
>> 2.     Attend an Orientation for the Voucher
>>
>> 3.     Complete a Port Request form
>>
>> 4.     HACC confirms that no debts are owed to HACC by the Household
>>
>> 5.     If there are no debts owed, HACC will port the voucher to
>> Multnomah County
>>
>> 6.     Multnomah County will have Jane and her household complete an
>> intake packet and attend Orientation in their County.
>>
>> 7.     After all of the above is completed, Jane and her Household would
>> be able to apply for housing with the voucher.
>>
>>
>>
>> March 31st is approaching quickly, and we need to confirm whether or not
>> Jane and Julio will be pursuing Blossom or a Tenant Protection Voucher.
>> Please let me know when I can speak to Jane about which she prefers.
>>
>>
>>
>> Thank you,
>>
>> * Ashley Ferron*
>> Pronouns (she/her)
>> Policy, Performance & Research Analyst, Sr.
>> Clackamas County Housing & Community Development Division (HCD)
>>
>> Clackamas County Department of Health, Housing & Human Services
>> P.O. Box 1510, 13930 S. Gain St, Oregon City, OR 97045-0510
>> <https://www.google.com/maps/search/13930+S.+Gain+St,+Oregon+City,+OR+97045-0510?entry=gmail&source=g>
>> Phone: 503-867-7487 – Text Friendly

>> *To:* HACC - Relocation <HACC-Relocation@clackamas.us>; Ferron, Ashley <
>> AFerron@clackamas.us>
>> *Cc:* Tilton, Kati <KTilton@clackamas.us>; blossom@quantumres.com
>> *Subject:* Re: Allegations of Fraud - JC Household
>>

>> * ------------------------------ *
>>
>>
>> ------------------------------
>>
>> Given the uncertainty and delay and changing expectations with regards to
>> the Blossom, and not wanting to expose my household to risks associated
>> with negligence, I had already submitted an application to a property
>> called Waterleaf apartments, which is located near OHSU hospital on south
>> waterfront, and the portland streetcar. The three criteria I wanted to
>> maintain were proximity to a hospital, public transit, and grocery, and
>> this was an apartment complex that seemed ideal given the circumstances.
>> However I do not know what the amount of the tenant protection voucher is,
>> and I need more information about the amount of the voucher, the
>> plausibility of getting that transferred to Multnomah County. I was told by
>> Janes representative payee for clackamas county, that she thought there was
>> some sort of danger to Jane, and her ability to have a residence, if I
>> tried to use the request / use the  tenant protection voucher at the
>> Waterleaf apartments, and transfer that to multnomah county. She also urged
>> to delay the Jan 1st deadline to file a lawsuit in the US district court,
>> however I did get very far in collecting the evidence which I would use,
>> should I need to go to the courts, to get the parties in this message to
>> state that they will comply with the laws of the united states.
>>
>> https://github.com/hallucinate-llc/HACC
>>
>>
>>

>>

>> This paperwork is factually incorrect, I have already submitted jane’s
>> application to blossom and I seem to recall a representation that the
>> blossom apartment was guaranteed for the residents here. Only recently had
>> this changed and I am learning for the first time that the place will not
>> be ready and that hillside park residents will be forced to move regardless
>> of the buildings being finished. Provide to me all documentation you have
>> regarding requirements and guidelines with regards to the blossom, because
>> it is my assumption that they want market renters, while also enjoying
>> government subsidies for the land and construction
>>
>>
>>
>> On Thu, Dec 11, 2025 at 5:44 PM benjamin barber <starworks5@gmail.com>
>> wrote:
>>
>> Great to hear from you but you have not done what you claimed to aim for
>> and I really get annoyed with people's behavior not aligned with my
>> expectations, and I would like to remind you that I’m not going to your
>> silly council it’s going to be filled in the us district court. I’m not
>> some crank like Julio is and I had another meeting with Stanford law school
>> computational law department, and have been working on a machine learning
>> system to automate DEI lawsuits for several years because of how much I
>> notice Oregon loves breaking laws. Feel free to ask some of my colleagues
>> if you don’t believe me
>>
>>
>>
>>
>>
>> On Thu, Dec 11, 2025 at 4:24 PM Tilton, Kati <KTilton@clackamas.us>
>> wrote:
>>
>> Hi Benjamin,
>>
>>
>>
>> My name is Kati; I’m the Operations Manager for the Housing Authority of
>> Clackamas County (HACC). I want to take a moment to follow up with you on
>> your most recent communication to the Housing Authority of Clackamas County
>> (HACC).
>>
>>
>>
>> Our goal is to ensure that we are supporting our households and providing
>> the highest level of assistance available. Additionally, we expect that
>> communication between the Housing Authority and those external to HACC
>> (participants, community partners, etc.) is safe and respectful.
>>
>>
>>
>> As such, I am assigning a single point of contact for you at the Housing
>> Authority. All staff have been instructed to direct communication from you
>> to Ashley Ferron who will work with the appropriate team/person(s) to
>> respond. All communication will be conducted in writing and will be sent to
>> and from Ashley Ferron. Communication will be limited to related details,
>> processes, procedures, and information specific to those activities your
>> mother’s household is engaged in with the Housing Authority of Clackamas
>> County.
>>
>>
>>
>> *Please note:* Housing Authority staff will not respond to threats of
>> litigation against the Housing Authority. If you believe that a member of
>> your household has committed fraud, you are welcome to share those details
>> with the Housing Authority so that we may follow up. If you feel that your
>> housing rights are being violated or feel that you are experiencing
>> discrimination, you are well within your right to contact the Fair Housing
>> Council of Oregon to discuss your options.
>>
>>
>>
>> Thank you,
>>
>>
>> Kati
>>
>>
>>
>>
>>
>> Kati Tilton
>>
>> 971-201-8582

>>
>> Your December 4th Lease adjustment is not well taken, and I am going
>> to file suit as soon as practical, but certainly within the end of the
>> month.
>>
>> There was a court hearing on 12/9/2025, whereby the judge after being
>> presented with an affidavit attached below, orally presented the facts
>> on the record:
>> Petitioner (me) is disabled, and petitioner was a victim of abuse.
>> This was memorialized in the order that I am going to send you to your
>> phone shortly. Moreover, I was told by the residents of 10165 SE A St
>> that they would be allowed to keep 2 dogs which are apparently larger
>> than the 25 lb rule, and therefore this constitutes a disparate
>> treatment, based on retaliatory premises and only subsequent to having
>> previously had a discussion with the housing authority of clackamas
>> county relocation specialist, whereby myself julio and jane
>> specifically mentioned concerns about our dog, and at which time there
>> was no mention of any 25lb rule. Julio Cortez also informed the court
>> that Housing Authority of Clackamas County was providing him with a
>> section 8 housing voucher, despite apparently defrauding the housing
>> authority of clackamas county, and no such affordances were provided
>> to me which amount to a disparate treatment. Lastly as a landlord
>> Housing Authority of Clackamas County is obligated to protect
>> individuals who are victims of domestic violation, and the
>> organization is also in violation of these statutes.
>>
>> https://www.washingtoncountyor.gov/housing/rights-survivors-violence
>>
>>
>> https://docs.google.com/document/d/1N5-CVIe5wjCUChiAr_7dahj_6UPMNhs86-4kzINN7pI/edit?usp=sharing
>>

>> constitution, and constitute fraud, and has authorized third parties to
>> file Qui Tam suits to recover expenses wrongfully paid by the US
>> government, and claimants to receive 30% of the amount that the federal
>> government recovers, and it is my intention to present those claims, and I
>> am hiring more people to assist me with the civil rights automation.

>> is an unacceptable position that you have put me in. I expect that this is
>> yet more negligence and disregard for laws and procedures, which I have
>> previously mentioned to your office with regards to the use of DEI
>> programs, to allocate housing based on race. Moreover, i have discovered
>> that the environmental impact study that was performed on the site was
>> negligently performed, and therefore all construction on the remaining
>> properties must cease, as a result of interfering with migratory birds
>> (canadian geese) under internationally protected treaties, and whose
>> migratory habitat (hillside PARK) was not preserved, e.g. having long
>> stretches of grass to consume, take off and land.
>> >>
>> >> You have until December 16th, to acknowledge receipt and provide a

body_html:
Sorry I’m on mobile, Waterleaf apartments wanted to know the voucher amount, she is scheduled for a tour on Thursday and has already signed the application and turned it in. Call her when you would like, but I’m inclined to leave clackamas county despite the fear mongering, especially about Julio coming back to live with us.

On Mon, Jan 12, 2026 at 9:59 AM benjamin barber <starworks5@gmail.com> wrote:
Jane doesn’t want to live with with Julio and has suggested that I take a restraining order against him on her behalf, and given that blossom told me the dog is not allowed and that the units would not be available that we chose the waterleaf apartments, and she has scheduled a tour of their place ( I went already) and a bus to take her there / ohsu campus , and I needed to confirm with waterless the amount of the voucher.

However Jamila ( clackamas county employee)  been scaring jane about her losing section 8 if she leaves clackamas county

On Mon, Jan 12, 2026 at 9:11 AM HACC - Relocation <HACC-Relocation@clackamas.us> wrote:

Hello,

Here are the steps Jane will need to take to receive a Tenant Protection Voucher and port to Multnomah County:

1.     Complete an intake packet and be screened by the intake team

2.     Attend an Orientation for the Voucher

3.     Complete a Port Request form

4.     HACC confirms that no debts are owed to HACC by the Household

5.     If there are no debts owed, HACC will port the voucher to Multnomah County

6.     Multnomah County will have Jane and her household complete an intake packet and attend Orientation in their County.

7.     After all of the above is completed, Jane and her Household would be able to apply for housing with the voucher.

 

March 31st is approaching quickly, and we need to confirm whether or not Jane and Julio will be pursuing Blossom or a Tenant Protection Voucher. Please let me know when I can speak to Jane about which she prefers.

 

Thank you,

Ashley Ferron
Pronouns (she/her)
Policy, Performance & Research Analyst, Sr.
Clackamas County Housing & Community Development Division (HCD)

Clackamas County Department of Health, Housing & Human Services
P.O. Box 1510, 13930 S. Gain St, Oregon City, OR 97045-0510
Phone: 503-867-7487 – Text Friendly

Hours of Operation:  Mon – Thur, 7:30 a.m. – 6 p.m.
www.clackamas.us/housingauthority

 

Follow Clackamas County: Facebook | Twitter | YouTube | Nextdoor

We are building a safe Clackamas County where all people thrive, are celebrated for their diverse identities, and know they belong.

 

From: benjamin barber <starworks5@gmail.com>
Sent: Tuesday, January 6, 2026 12:02 PM
To: HACC - Relocation <HACC-Relocation@clackamas.us>; Ferron, Ashley <AFerron@clackamas.us>
Cc: Tilton, Kati <KTilton@clackamas.us>; blossom@quantumres.com
Subject: Re: Allegations of Fraud - JC Household

 

Warning: External email. Be cautious opening attachments and links.

 

Given the uncertainty and delay and changing expectations with regards to the Blossom, and not wanting to expose my household to risks associated with negligence, I had already submitted an application to a property called Waterleaf apartments, which is located near OHSU hospital on south waterfront, and the portland streetcar. The three criteria I wanted to maintain were proximity to a hospital, public transit, and grocery, and this was an apartment complex that seemed ideal given the circumstances. However I do not know what the amount of the tenant protection voucher is, and I need more information about the amount of the voucher, the plausibility of getting that transferred to Multnomah County. I was told by Janes representative payee for clackamas county, that she thought there was some sort of danger to Jane, and her ability to have a residence, if I tried to use the request / use the  tenant protection voucher at the Waterleaf apartments, and transfer that to multnomah county. She also urged to delay the Jan 1st deadline to file a lawsuit in the US district court, however I did get very far in collecting the evidence which I would use, should I need to go to the courts, to get the parties in this message to state that they will comply with the laws of the united states.

https://github.com/hallucinate-llc/HACC

Warning: External email. Be cautious opening attachments and links.

 

 

This paperwork is factually incorrect, I have already submitted jane’s application to blossom and I seem to recall a representation that the blossom apartment was guaranteed for the residents here. Only recently had this changed and I am learning for the first time that the place will not be ready and that hillside park residents will be forced to move regardless of the buildings being finished. Provide to me all documentation you have regarding requirements and guidelines with regards to the blossom, because it is my assumption that they want market renters, while also enjoying government subsidies for the land and construction

 

On Thu, Dec 11, 2025 at 5:44 PM benjamin barber <starworks5@gmail.com> wrote:

Great to hear from you but you have not done what you claimed to aim for and I really get annoyed with people's behavior not aligned with my expectations, and I would like to remind you that I’m not going to your silly council it’s going to be filled in the us district court. I’m not some crank like Julio is and I had another meeting with Stanford law school computational law department, and have been working on a machine learning system to automate DEI lawsuits for several years because of how much I notice Oregon loves breaking laws. Feel free to ask some of my colleagues if you don’t believe me

 

 

On Thu, Dec 11, 2025 at 4:24 PM Tilton, Kati <KTilton@clackamas.us> wrote:

Hi Benjamin,

 

My name is Kati; I’m the Operations Manager for the Housing Authority of Clackamas County (HACC). I want to take a moment to follow up with you on your most recent communication to the Housing Authority of Clackamas County (HACC).

 

Our goal is to ensure that we are supporting our households and providing the highest level of assistance available. Additionally, we expect that communication between the Housing Authority and those external to HACC (participants, community partners, etc.) is safe and respectful.

 

As such, I am assigning a single point of contact for you at the Housing Authority. All staff have been instructed to direct communication from you to Ashley Ferron who will work with the appropriate team/person(s) to respond. All communication will be conducted in writing and will be sent to and from Ashley Ferron. Communication will be limited to related details, processes, procedures, and information specific to those activities your mother’s household is engaged in with the Housing Authority of Clackamas County.

 

Please note: Housing Authority staff will not respond to threats of litigation against the Housing Authority. If you believe that a member of your household has committed fraud, you are welcome to share those details with the Housing Authority so that we may follow up. If you feel that your housing rights are being violated or feel that you are experiencing discrimination, you are well within your right to contact the Fair Housing Council of Oregon to discuss your options.

 

Thank you,


Kati

 

 

Kati Tilton

971-201-8582

Operations Manager, Housing Authority of Clackamas County

that they would be allowed to keep 2 dogs which are apparently larger
than the 25 lb rule, and therefore this constitutes a disparate

previously had a discussion with the housing authority of clackamas
county relocation specialist, whereby myself julio and jane
specifically mentioned concerns about our dog, and at which time there
was no mention of any 25lb rule. Julio Cortez also informed the court
that Housing Authority of Clackamas County was providing him with a
section 8 housing voucher, despite apparently defrauding the housing
authority of clackamas county, and no such affordances were provided
to me which amount to a disparate treatment. Lastly as a landlord
Housing Authority of Clackamas County is obligated to protect
individuals who are victims of domestic violation, and the
organization is also in violation of these statutes.
